name: Lumen
description: A stripped-down, lightning-fast "micro-framework" version of Laravel specifically meant for APIs and microservices. Superseded by Laravel Octane and the sheer speed of modern PHP.

name: Laravel Mix
description: A beloved, fluent API wrapper around Webpack for asset compilation. It made Webpack actually enjoyable to use, but was eventually replaced by Vite as the default frontend bundler.

name: Laravel Elixir
description: The predecessor to Laravel Mix. It was a fluent wrapper around Gulp instead of Webpack. Killed as the frontend ecosystem shifted away from task runners.

name: Laravel Homestead
description: The official Vagrant box that provided a standardized local development environment for Laravel without requiring you to install PHP or Nginx on your machine.
A simple frontend package that provided basic Bootstrap, Vue, and React scaffolding along with the classic make:auth commands. Replaced by Laravel Jetstream and Breeze.
name: Laravel UI

description: Originally, Laravel Cashier supported Stripe and Braintree. The Braintree integration was extracted and subsequently abandoned so the team could focus purely on Stripe and Paddle.
name: Laravel Cashier Braintree

description: Spark Classic shipped with massive amounts of Vue.js scaffolding and essentially dictated your entire frontend architecture. Replaced by a more isolated standalone billing portal.
name: Laravel Spark Classic

description: The original way Laravel handled application testing before Laravel 5.4. Replaced by the much cleaner HTTP Testing API and Laravel Dusk for actual browser-based automation.
name: BrowserKit Testing

description: Originally Illuminate/Html, it was removed from core and maintained closely by the community. Eventually abandoned as pure HTML and Blade components became preferred.
name: Laravel Collective (HTML & Forms)

description: A featherweight authentication system for SPAs. The package wasn't killed, but the name was: weeks after launch, Laravel was forced to rename it to Sanctum over a trademark dispute.
name: Laravel Airlock
